world.title: Two more Indonesian peacekeepers killed in Lebanon, bringing total to three
world.description: The two Indonesian soldiers were killed "when an explosion of unknown origin destroyed their vehicle" in South Lebanon on Monday (Mar 30), the United Nations (UN) said. A day earlier, another Indonesian soldier was killed in a separate incident.

world.title: Industrial fire breaks out near ADAMA facility in Israel
world.description: Fire at ADAMA's southern Israel plant prompted a hazardous materials warning as crews worked to contain the blaze.

world.title: Groups criticize eased rules on hiring migrant domestic workers
world.description: Advocacy groups on Tuesday criticized the central government's easing of rules on hiring migrant domestic workers, saying the move will do little to address Taiwan's low birth rate, while welcoming Taipei's new policy allowing parents to work shorter hours.

world.title: Trump signals halt, Netanyahu stance, Israel law live feed
world.description: Trump Iran War: Trump says he may halt US military action despite Hormuz closure, as Netanyahu refuses timeline and Israel clears death penalty law for Palestinians. Follow live updates on conflict, tensions, Strait of Hormuz crisis and global response.

world.title: Liberia stalls on lead rules, with children at risk
world.description: Delayed enforcement of lead paint rules leaves Liberian children exposed to toxic risks.

world.description: Pre-Iran war, and before 2023 Houthi strikes on shipping lanes, ten to 12 per cent of the world's seaborne crude - i.e., between 8.7 and 9.3 million barrels - shipped via the Bab al-Mandab daily.

world.title: Grants available to Enhance Wellbeing and Resilience of Communities in Australia
world.description: Deadline: 24-Jun-2026 The Strengthening Rural Communities – Small and Vital program funds grassroots initiatives that enhance wellbeing, resilience, and community connection in remote, rural, and regional Australia. Grants of up to $10,000 (small) and $50,000 (larger leverage) support locally driven projects that improve social, cultural, and digital outcomes for diverse and vulnerable groups. business.title: DOST opens hub to push commercialization of innovations
business.description: The Department of Science and Technology (DOST) has launched the PROPEL Business Hub and Exhibit Center in Taguig City to fast-track the commercialization of Filipino innovations, connect startups with scientists and investors, and strengthen the country’s science, technology, and innovation ecosystem across key sectors like agriculture, health, renewable energy, and digital solutions.

business.title: LinkedIn to End Spontaneous Live Streams, Introduces Mandatory Scheduling
business.description: The platform’s new scheduled-only live format is designed to boost discoverability, improve audience turnout, and push creators toward more deliberate event planning.

science.title: New rain frogs discovered in Ecuador face immediate extinction risk
science.description: New frog species found on a single Ecuador mountain face immediate extinction risk, revealing both hidden biodiversity and growing threats.

science.title: Quantum Gases Recreate Extreme Waves Seen In Oceans And Optics
science.description: Previously confined to unpredictable ocean swells or complex optical systems, extreme waves are now appearing on demand in the laboratory. Atomic gases, carefully manipulated to mimic these natural phenomena, have yielded the first controllable creation of a Peregrine soliton, a notoriously large and abrupt wave. This advance promises a new era for studying these elusive events and understanding their behaviour across diverse physical systems.

science.title: Scientists discover potentially huge freshwater reservoir hidden beneath Great Salt Lake
science.description: Researchers have found a layer of fresh water beneath Utah's Great Salt Lake that reaches up to 2.5 miles deep and could turn out to be as big, or bigger, than the lake.

science.title: LIDAR and gaussian splatting: Advancements in digital forest twin validation
science.description: Scientists explore the utility of XGRIDS Portal Cam technology for creating high-definition digital forest twins for validating aerial LiDAR forest inventories

science.title: NASA begins the countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years

science.title: How Artemis II expands the Apollo moon mission legacy
science.description: The Artemis II mission will take astronauts beyond low-Earth orbit for the first time in 50 years. This next era of the U.S. space program marks a step toward building a permanent presence in outer space.

science.title: Turning muscles into motors gives static organs new life
science.description: What if a technology could reanimate parts of the body that have lost their connection to the brain—like a bladder that can no longer empty due to a spinal cord injury, or intestines that can't push food forward due to Crohn's disease? What if this technology could also send sensations such as hunger or touch back to the brain?

science.title: Stanford scientists create shape-shifting material that changes color and texture like an octopus
science.description: A new shape-shifting material can change both its texture and color in seconds, inspired by the camouflage abilities of octopuses. By precisely controlling how a polymer swells with water, researchers can create detailed, reversible patterns at the nanoscale. The material can even mimic realistic surfaces and dynamically adjust how it reflects light. In the future, AI could allow it to automatically blend into its surroundings.

ai.title: How Iran War Is Sending Shockwaves Through Wall Street: Oil Prices, AI Panic Push S&P 500 To Worst Quarter Since Pandemic Era
ai.description: Wall Street is heading for its worst quarter in four years as investors pull back amid rising inflation fears and geopolitical tensions. The S&P 500 is set to fall about 7% in Q1 2026, pressured by soaring oil prices, AI disruption worries, and a sharp sell-off in Big Tech.

ai.title: Tax Season 2026: Government Notifies New ITR Forms; Here is the July 31 Deadline Guide
ai.description: The Finance Ministry has officially notified the Income Tax Return (ITR) forms for Assessment Year 2026-27. With the July 31 deadline set, the new forms incorporate key shifts under the Income-tax Act 2025, requiring taxpayers to disclose more granular data on global assets and AI-driven income.

ai.title: Pioneering Innovation in Telecom Power: Huawei Wins Global Best Practices Award 2025
ai.description: SHENZHEN, China, March 31, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Recently, Huawei Site Power Facility won the Global Best Practices Award 2025 from Frost & Sullivan for its continuous technological innovation and industry leadership. This award recognizes Huawei's outstanding contributions to the application of Virtual Power Plant (VPP) technologies and AI-powered green site solutions, highlighting its industry leadership in driving global operators' transition from traditional energy consumers to energy prosumers.

ai.title: Japan gets an alternative iOS game store.
ai.description: Aptoide’s AppArena is now available in Japan as an Apple Store alternative. It comes after regulators required Apple and Google to support third-party app marketplaces and payment systems. AppArena features AI-assisted discovery of apps and games, cashback rewards, and 15-minute game trials.
